postmanEvidence: Postman Desktop resolves Package Library and external package imports before script execution and exposes prepared packages through the sandbox require surface.
The audited postman-sandbox package model supports synchronous CommonJS-style module loading and per-module cache behavior inside the sandbox.

observedPostmanPackageModel.packageLibraryResolution: Postman Desktop resolves Package Library and external package imports before script execution; scripts consume already prepared packages through the sandbox require surface.
observedPostmanPackageModel.sandboxBoundary: Package loading happens inside postman-sandbox script execution, while host Node, Electron, filesystem, process, shell, native modules, path specifiers, URL specifiers, registry fetches, and runtime installs are not direct script capabilities.
commonJsExpectations: partially initialized exports during supported circular dependencies

postmeterImplementation.reviewedCache: PostMeter stores reviewed exact team, npm, and checksum-verified JSR package entries with SHA-256 integrity over normalized file maps, package metadata, and entrypoint metadata. Legacy single-source entries remain supported with the original source digest.
postmeterImplementation.loaderSemantics: The script VM loader resolves package entrypoints from explicit entrypoint metadata, package.json main/browser/module/exports fields, and index fallbacks; supports relative file, directory package.json, extension, .json, .js, .cjs, and .mjs resolution; pre-caches module records for circular dependencies; exposes module, exports, module.require, require.resolve, __filename, and __dirname; and keeps default export object patterns intact.
postmeterImplementation.dependencyPolicy: Reviewed packages may synchronously require only Postman bundled facades or reviewed dependencies declared in the package entry. Dependency aliases and reviewed dependency subpaths map package-local bare specifiers to exact reviewed package file-map entries without giving scripts Node or registry resolution.
postmeterImplementation.securityBoundary: The loader rejects host Node modules outside the approved Postman facade list, node: specifiers, absolute or parent-escaping paths, URLs, native modules, unreviewed packages, duplicate cache entries, missing dependencies, oversized packages, and integrity mismatches.

focusedTests: test/electron/scriptRuntime.test.js supports multi-file reviewed CommonJS packages with metadata, cache, circular dependencies, and default interop
test/electron/sandboxPackageFetcher.test.js normalizes reviewed npm/JSR/team package files and bundle integrity
test/electron/sandboxPackageIpc.test.js preserves fetched file maps, package metadata, dependency aliases, and integrity
test/electron/postmanSandboxCorpus.test.js executes the golden corpus reviewed multi-file package import
test/electron/postmanSandboxAdversarial.test.js rejects package-loader abuse without host Node or unreviewed package access

limitations: This artifact is a source and implementation audit. Package Library UI behavior and online fetch/review workflow evidence is tracked separately by require.pm.online-fetch-review.
This artifact covers reviewed CommonJS-compatible package execution. Native ESM execution for JSR packages is tracked separately by require.pm.jsr-esm-module-semantics.
PostMeter intentionally preserves the reviewed-cache security model instead of giving scripts direct npm, JSR, filesystem, or Node resolution.
notes: This artifact clears the CommonJS bundle-semantics claim blocker by tying the Postman Desktop package model to PostMeter's reviewed multi-file CommonJS loader, integrity model, dependency policy, focused tests, adversarial denials, and golden corpus coverage.
